Title: Genetic basis for glandular trichome formation in cotton

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Seed-specific silencing of CYP706B1 significantly reduced gossypol levels in transgenic seeds but did not affect lysigenous gland formation.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed development; seed-specific RNAi driven by globulin promoter
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used to demonstrate that gossypol biosynthesis is uncoupled from gland formation. Encodes (+)-δ-cadinene-8-hydroxylase, a P450 monooxygenase in the gossypol biosynthesis pathway.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Lysigenous glands in aerial tissues and seeds
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cotton defense against pests, diseases and abiotic stresses; reduction in seed enables improved edibility/protein and oil utilization
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supports engineering low-gossypol seeds without disrupting gland formation by seed-specific silencing of a biosynthetic gene.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Seed-specific RNAi transgenics; metabolite profiling by HPLC; gland phenotype observation
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced defensive terpenoid in seeds; no gland morphology change reported
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP706B1 silencing in seeds significantly lowered gossypol content. 2. Lysigenous glands still formed normally in transgenic cotton. 3. Therefore gossypol biosynthesis and gland morphogenesis are genetically uncoupled.

Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: GenBank PRJNA265955 (CDN-1 discussed as downstream target/promoter context)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cotton defense against pests, diseases and abiotic stresses; reduced seed gossypol improves nutritional utility
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyses the first committed step in gossypol biosynthesis, forming (+)-δ-cadinene from farnesyl diphosphate.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Silencing of CDN reduces gossypol biosynthesis but does not affect gland formation; its promoter likely contains G-box motifs potentially responsive to GoPGF.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned as prior evidence and as a possible downstream terpenoid synthase target in the GoPGF regulatory network rather than directly functionally dissected in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Downregulated terpenoid synthase genes were detected in GoPGF-silenced leaves; specific CDN expression validation cited from previous work and promoter context discussed
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CDN catalyses the first step of gossypol biosynthesis. 2. Previous silencing showed terpenoid aldehyde synthesis can be reduced without altering gland formation. 3. CDN-1 promoter contains a G-box motif that may be bound by GoPGF, linking gland regulation to gossypol pathway transcription.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Prior gene silencing evidence referenced; RNA-seq/qPCR downstream expression context; promoter motif binding inference via GoPGF yeast one-hybrid to G-box
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Seed-specific suppression is a validated strategy to reduce seed gossypol while preserving glands in vegetative tissues.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms early pathway intermediate required for target product

Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: GenBank PRJNA265955; reference genome alignment to G. hirsutum TM-1 PRJNA248163
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Gossypol contributes to defense against pests, diseases and abiotic stresses; reducing seed gossypol could improve cottonseed use as food protein and oil
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Positive transcriptional regulation of gland cell specification/differentiation and indirect/direct activation of gossypol-related genes; binds G-box motif
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Map-based cloning; co-segregation analysis; qRT-PCR; VIGS silencing; histology; HPLC metabolite profiling; subcellular localization; yeast one-hybrid; RNA-seq and qPCR validation
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Lysigenous glands of cotton aerial tissues and seeds
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Proposed feedback from gland formation regulator to gossypol biosynthetic pathway via regulation of TPS/WRKY genes
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Dominant glandless allele in Hai-1 is associated with reduced expression and an Ala43Val substitution; recessive glandless alleles gl2 and gl3 carry single-nucleotide insertions causing premature stop codons in homoeologous copies GhPGF_A12m and GhPGF_D12m. GoPGF belongs to a distinct bHLH clade associated with glandular trichome formation.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major positive regulator of gland formation, the storage structure for gossypol; silencing causes glandless phenotype and >93% reduction of hemigossypol and gossypol in leaves
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Constitutively expressed in most organs including root, stem and leaf; reduced in dominant glandless Hai-1 and F1; very low in recessive mutant alleles
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A key target for developing glandless seed/glanded plant cotton or delayed gland morphogenesis strategies to improve cottonseed nutritional value while retaining defense in vegetative tissues.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: G. hirsutum TM-1; G. barbadense Hai-1, Giza 45, Hai7124
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Suppressing GoPGF removes glands and strongly decreases defensive sesquiterpenes, potentially compromising pest/pathogen resistance
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GoPGF encodes a nucleus-localized bHLH transcription factor and is the causative gene underlying dominant and recessive glandless phenotypes. 2. Silencing GoPGF abolishes gland formation in new tissues and reduces hemigossypol/gossypol by >93%. 3. GoPGF is JA inducible, binds G-box motifs, and is associated with activation of TPS/WRKY/JA-related genes linked to gland formation and gossypol biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Downstream positive effect on terpenoid synthase genes (including CDN-1 context), WRKY genes, MYB genes, and JA signaling-related genes; likely regulates JAZ, WRKYs, and TPSs
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Basic helix-loop-helix (bHLH) transcription factor; N-terminal motif Pfam14215; C-terminal helix-loop-helix DNA-binding domain Pfam00010
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Constitutive in root, stem and leaf in glanded cotton
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Glandular trichome/gland formation and associated sesquiterpene biosynthesis/storage
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter analyzed; no differential GUS activity detected between TM-1 and Hai-1 promoter fragments in tobacco transient assay
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: TPS genes; WRKY genes; JAZ-related genes; CDN-1 promoter context via G-box
